one_line_description: Open-source Python CLI that hunts for a username across 400+ social networks by probing public profile URLs.
auth_summary: No authentication at all: it is a local CLI tool, not a SaaS. It requires no API keys or logins for itself or for the sites it checks — it just requests public profile URLs and inspects responses.
access_notes: Free and open source (pipx/pip install sherlock-project, git clone, or the official Docker image). No account, credentials, or vendor relationship involved.
api_surface_summary: Single-purpose CLI: given username(s), it checks 400+ sites and reports where the handle exists, with flags for timeouts, output files, NSFW inclusion, etc. No hosted API of any kind.
main_blocker: No hosted API — Composio would have to run the Python CLI in its own execution environment and parse CLI output; results depend on brittle site-detection heuristics.
agent_notes: Recorded honestly as an OSS CLI, not a SaaS. Several community MCP wrappers exist (Burnsedia/sherlock-mcp, qKitNp/sherlock_mcp, p1atdev/sherlock-mcp); none are official. Note: fewsats/sherlock-mcp is an unrelated 'Sherlock Domains' product. A wrapped toolkit is feasible but requires self-hosting the tool and accepting slow multi-site scans and false positives/negatives.
